subUnits.title: 1.1 Counting & Writing Large Numbers
notes: Whole numbers are counting numbers: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4... and so on.
Large numbers are grouped in sets of THREE digits from right to left: Ones, Thousands, Millions, Billions, Trillions.
Example: 4,502,000 = Four million, five hundred and two thousand.
Commas (or spaces) help us read large numbers easily.
Practice: Count in tens, hundreds, and thousands up to 1,000,000.

quiz.q: What is the place value of 7 in 3,750,000?
quiz.explanation: The digit 7 is in the hundred thousands place.

quiz.q: Which number represents 'Two billion, four hundred million'?

subUnits.title: 1.2 Reading Large Numbers in Everyday Life
notes: We use large numbers every day: population, money, distances, school fees.
Example: Nigeria's population is about 220,000,000 (Two hundred and twenty million).
When reading large numbers, say each group name: 'Three million, four hundred and fifty thousand'.
Tip: Read from LEFT to RIGHT, saying the group name after each 3-digit block.
Practice: Read numbers from newspapers, price tags, or school records.

opts: Twelve million, three hundred forty-five thousand, six hundred seventy-eight
One million, two hundred thirty-four thousand, five hundred sixty-seven
Twelve billion, three hundred forty-five million
One hundred twenty-three million, four hundred fifty-six thousand

quiz.q: Which is the correct way to write 'Five million, thirty-two thousand'?
In real life, where might you see large numbers?
quiz.explanation: Large numbers appear in population, money, distances, and many daily situations.

subUnits.title: 1.3 Roman Numeral System
notes: Romans used letters to represent numbers:
• Add when a smaller/equal letter follows: VI = 5+1 = 6
• Subtract when smaller precedes larger: IV = 5-1 = 4
• Never repeat V, L, or D. Repeat I, X, C, M max 3 times.

quiz.q: What is the Roman numeral for 49?
Which letter represents 50 in Roman numerals?
